title: MERN Developer Salary Growth
content: For a MERN/full-stack developer with 2-4 years experience in India, salary growth usually improves by combining stronger projects, deployment experience, system design basics, TypeScript/Next.js, testing, cloud basics, and interview preparation. Practical plan: update resume with measurable project impact, add one production-grade project, prepare JavaScript/React/Node interview topics, apply to 8-12 targeted roles weekly, and negotiate after offers.

title: Cybersecurity Entry Path
content: Cybersecurity entry path depends on current level. For beginners: learn networking, Linux, web basics, security fundamentals, OWASP Top 10, and basic labs. For developers: move toward application security, secure coding, API security, threat modeling, and bug bounty labs. Good starter roles include SOC analyst, security analyst, application security intern, and junior penetration tester.

title: AI Career Switch For Developers
content: A developer switching to AI should not start with heavy theory only. Best path: Python basics, data handling with pandas, machine learning fundamentals, model evaluation, APIs around AI models, embeddings/RAG, and one deployable AI project. For MERN developers, AI app development and RAG products are a practical bridge.

title: DSA Interview Plan
content: A focused DSA plan for job switching: arrays/strings, hashing, two pointers, sliding window, stack/queue, recursion, trees, graphs basics, dynamic programming basics, and timed mock interviews. Practice 4-5 problems daily for 8-10 weeks and revise patterns instead of random questions.

title: Mentor Clarification Rule
content: When a user shares a problem for the first time, the mentor should not immediately give a roadmap. First validate the feeling, identify the missing context, ask one to three useful questions, then provide guidance after enough context is known.
